title: Build progress via Generator
body:
* Present service progress to caller when using Build().
* Update changed black options

Closes #171

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body: @msisj How does this look? You should have access to the build progress and it does not break the current call API.

@jwhonce 

Nice! Your solution works.
I must have missed that combination (`stream=True` and no `api.stream_frames`) during testing - my bad.

To be honest, in order to keep compatibility with docker-py, give user access to real-time logs and to not break current API I'd : 

1. Add `stream=False` option to `pull` method
2. set stream=stream in `/images/pull` REST method
3. `if stream is True` -> `return response.iter_lines()` so that generator is returned giving user option to parse it in real time (that's how docker-py handled that)

(Tested that solution locally and it seems to be working just fine)

@msisj  I was writing to their higher level API which does not have the `stream` keyword [documented](https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/stable/images.html#docker.models.images.ImageCollection.pull). I am happy to go with the solution you describe as it does not break the API either.

@jwhonce 
As I've worked quite a bit with docker-py I'm kind of familiar with their documentation, which has weird interface (regular one and the other for low level api) for documentation.
To get to the real stuff one has to click `Low-level API` where we can find https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#docker.api.image.ImageApiMixin.pull which has these options described.

Also I can see that your `Gating test` has failed on pylint. I'm having similar issue under https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/172 . What I can see is that for some reason pylint fails on files which were not touched in the PR. My guess it that some code was merged, which introduced these failures, without passing pylint. Could you take a look at it?

@jwhonce 
Also I've got another question, is there a possibility to get the actual progress of the image being pulled (downloaded/full layer size) ? (like we get in podman pull command; docker-py actually showed that level of details) : 
```
Copying blob 2f408cfwf45f [=======>------------------------------] 104.9MiB / 530.8MiB
```
